title: Modular document rendering for CSV and source files in Web UI
description:
Improve the Web UI document viewer so issue-linked CSV and source-oriented artifacts render in a richer, format-aware way. The implementation must stay modular so future formats can be added by registering a renderer instead of adding format-specific branching to the viewer shell.

## Background

The current Web UI document pipeline supports HTML and Markdown rendering, but the `../gf2` issues used for real-world review also attach `.csv`, `.txt`, `.rs`, and `.cpp` documents. Those files currently fall back to generic text handling even when the data would be easier to inspect through format-aware rendering.

The agreed v1 behavior is:
- `.csv`: sortable table, sticky header, inline rich/raw toggle, search highlighting, capped rich preview for large files
- `.rs` / `.cpp`: syntax highlighting, line numbers, inline rich/raw toggle, search highlighting, capped rich preview for large files
- `.txt`: plain-text mode with wrapping controls, line numbers, inline rich/raw toggle, search highlighting, capped rich preview for large files

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Issue-linked `.csv` documents render through a dedicated modular renderer with sortable columns, a sticky header, inline rich/raw switching, search highlighting, and capped rich preview behavior for large files.
- [ ] Issue-linked `.txt`, `.rs`, and `.cpp` documents render through modular text/code presentation with the agreed per-format behavior and inline raw fallback.
- [ ] Renderer dispatch remains data-driven through registry metadata and path/content matching rather than hardcoded per-format branching in `DocumentViewer`.
- [ ] Existing Markdown and HTML document rendering behavior remains intact, and unknown text-like formats still degrade gracefully.
- [ ] The work is broken into child issues with explicit success criteria and gates so implementation can proceed in an auditable order.

## Notes

This work is tracked under `6d0128ae` as a focused usability slice for issue-linked documents in the Web UI. The design and task breakdown are created as part of the first child task.
